title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Type Recall
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs node http primitives, connections, protocols, timeouts, and shutdown from bounded code and request evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.
Configure requestTimeout, headersTimeout, keepAliveTimeout, keepAliveTimeoutBuffer, maxHeadersCount, and maximum header size from a threat and latency budget.
Distinguish HTTP/1.1, Node HTTP/2 compatibility behavior, ALPN, TLS, upgrades, CONNECT, and the explicit external boundary for HTTP/3.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Node Request Response Streams
steps.instruction:
Order the Node Request Response Streams Node and Express server HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Node Request Response Streams.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Node Request Response Streams assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Node and Express server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Node Request Response Streams, keep Node Request Response Streams connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A review team handles case 6257 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; record only bounded redacted diagnostic fields; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace IncomingMessage and ServerResponse as streams with header commitment, body ownership, backpressure, abort, error, and close lifetimes.

content.text: Order work so Node Request Response Streams has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
